one_liner: Aircall is a cloud phone/call-center platform offering a paid REST API (Basic Auth or OAuth2) for calls, contacts, users, numbers, and webhooks.

auth_methods.notes: HTTP Basic Auth using api_id and api_token generated in the Aircall Dashboard; Authorization header set to Base64(api_id:api_token). Intended for customers building for their own account.
Bearer token based OAuth2 for multi-customer integrations, e.g. Aircall App Marketplace partners; requires partner account signup.

self_serve_notes: No free tier includes API access; Aircall is a paid subscription (Essentials, Professional, Custom). Basic Auth credentials (api_id/api_token) can be self-generated via the Dashboard by any paying customer, but OAuth2 for multi-customer partner integrations requires applying via the Aircall partner/technology signup process. Advanced API support reserved for Custom/enterprise plans.

api_surface.notes: REST API base https://api.aircall.io/v1 covering calls, contacts, users, numbers, teams, webhooks; also supports webhooks for real-time events.

main_blocker: Requires an active paid Aircall subscription to obtain API credentials (Basic Auth) or partner approval for OAuth2 multi-customer integrations.

evidence.supports: Describes REST API, webhooks, and OAuth vs Basic Auth authentication choices.
Details REST endpoints (calls, users, contacts, numbers, teams, webhooks) and Basic Auth/OAuth2 mechanics.
Shows paid subscription tiers; no free tier for API access.
Example of existing third-party Aircall API/MCP-adjacent tooling.

flags: No official Aircall-maintained MCP server; existing MCP servers are community/third-party (e.g., themobilefirstco, Pipedream, viaSocket).
API access gated behind paid subscription plans; free trial may allow limited testing but no persistent free tier.
